tool_args.label: Long Island Rail Road Financial and Ridership Reports – February 2025

payload.title: Long Island Rail Road Financial and Ridership Reports – February 2025

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Total expenses before non-cash liability adjustments of $389.9 million
Total headcount was 7,902 which was 105 positions lower than the budget
The February Adjusted Farebox Operating Ratio was 32.6%
The February Adjusted Cost per Passenger was $28.42
The year-to-date February Revenue per Passenger was $8.49
LIRR revenue totaled $47.8 million in February 2025

tool_args.label: Farebox Revenue favorable due to higher paid ridership

payload.claim_text: Farebox Revenues were $6.9 million favorable to the budget due to higher paid ridership, partially offset by lower yield per passenger

tool_args.label: Overtime unfavorable due to weather and maintenance

payload.claim_text: Overtime expenses were $5.4 million unfavorable (higher weather-related overtime, and maintenance)

tool_args.label: Payroll favorable due to new hires

payload.claim_text: Payroll expenses were $2.7 million favorable (lower average pay rates reflecting new hires)

Materials and Supplies were $2.5 million unfavorable primarily due to the timing of modifications and RCM activity for revenue fleet and miscellaneous inventory adjustment, partially offset by right-of-way materials
Depreciation and Other were $10.2 million unfavorable (primarily GASB 87 and depreciation)

tool_args.label: Adjusted Farebox Operating Ratio YTD Table

payload.description: Ridership metric reported for Long Island Rail Road

evidence_refs.source_quote: ridership was 11.7 million which was 8.8% above 2024

payload.claim_text: The Standard Farebox Operating Ratio and Cost Per Passenger indicators reflect MTA-wide adopted calculations that exclude non-cash liability adjustments: Depreciation, Other Post Employment Benefits and Environmental Remediation (GASB-49).

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Standard Farebox Operating Ratio and Cost Per Passenger indicators reflect MTA-wide adopted calculations that exclude non-cash liability adjustments

payload.claim_text: Adjusted Fare Box Operating Ratio and Cost Per Passenger indicators have been adjusted for comparability between the Long Island Rail Road and Metro-North Railroad and are being presented only at the railroad operating committees. Adjustments include: Inclusion of Other Operating Revenue, Removal of OPEB Current Payment expenses for retirees, and Removal of the UAAL associated with the LIRR's closed pension plan.
payload.description: LIRR adjusted performance indicator methodology footnote

evidence_refs.source_quote: Adjusted Fare Box Operating Ratio and Cost Per Passenger indicators have been adjusted for comparability between the Long Island Rail Road and Metro-North Railroad

evidence_refs.source_quote: Commutation ridership of 4.7 million was 8.6% above 2024 and 22.0% above the budget
